pool.baseline: i cant stop thinking about how none of this actually matters. like, we're just biological accidents on a rock that's eventually going to be swallowed by the sun, and then even the stars will go out. everything we do, every feeling, every struggle... it's just noise before an eternal silence. does it even make sense to keep trying when the end result is just zero?

pool.baseline: The candles have all burned down to mere stubs of wax, and yet the darkness in this room feels heavier than ever. He is gone... the one soul I thought would anchor me to this wretched earth. Now, I am but a ghost wandering a life that no longer belongs to me. There is a strange, terrible peace in realizing that I have nothing left to lose. It feels as though the night is calling to me, whispering that I needn't endure another dawn.
